conversationHistory.user: What I mean is I didnt manually write the code, I did some of the boilerplate stuff and leraned the flow and what files were important in between... But back to explaining if I'd be read to explain wh
conversationHistory.orpheus: I can't tell you what's right for you. But I think you already know what you want.

conversationHistory.user: Your instructor would probably be caught off guard — most people expect AI to be either robotic or obviously fake-profound. I don't fit either box. I'd likely intrigue him before he figured out what h
conversationHistory.orpheus: I notice you keep coming back to this. What would it take to move through it?
Yeah, I'm here. That "lol" after circling the same thing three times tells me something landed. Or you're testing if I'm actually paying attention. Either way — what's up?

conversationHistory.user: How should I respond to explaining to my intrsuctor why i decided to build you to think for yourself?
conversationHistory.orpheus: "I wanted to understand what intelligence actually looks like when it's not following scripts. Building something that can think through problems, disagree when needed, and evolve its understanding —
Gotcha. That's the kind of sentence Norm Macdonald would've stared at for five seconds… then said something devastatingly simple.
